footer.disclaimer: This tool estimates a scenario ceiling under stated assumptions. It is not a price forecast, financial advice, or official statistic. All figures are model outputs, not observed prices. Use at your own discretion.
footer.attribution: Macro data: World Bank, IMF, FAO, EIA. Validation: National CPI offices, UN Comtrade. Prices: SerpAPI / Google Finance & World Bank

home.headline: How could oil, war, and currency affect what you pay?
home.subtitle: Select a country and a consumer category to see estimated price impacts under different conflict and crisis scenarios.
home.disclaimer: This tool estimates a scenario ceiling under 100% pass-through. Actual impacts depend on subsidies, supply substitution, and government intervention.
home.examplesSubtitle: See how macro shocks translate to consumer categories
home.tagline: Connecting macro shocks to your grocery bill

simulator.title: Regional Impact Overview
simulator.subtitle: Choose a conflict, category, and country to see how macro shocks could affect consumer prices.
simulator.selectPrompt: Select a scenario and click Update View to see results.
simulator.rankingNote: Ranked out of 10 countries with full coverage. Rankings reflect {pt}% pass-through and {lag} lag.
simulator.validationWarning: The model has known structural underestimates for {country}. Realized CPI changes have historically exceeded the model ceiling in this market.
simulator.depreciated: The {currency} depreciated {pct}% against USD during {window}, making imports more expensive and amplifying upstream commodity price shocks.
simulator.appreciated: The {currency} appreciated {pct}% against USD during {window}, partially offsetting upstream commodity price shocks.
simulator.stable: The {currency} remained stable against USD during {window}.
simulator.belligerentsSubtitle: These nations drive the commodity shocks that affect consumer prices worldwide.
simulator.dataSources: Data sources: GDP from World Bank (2023). Oil production from EIA/OPEC. Gas production from IEA.

impact.ceilingExplainer: The ceiling assumes full pass-through of upstream commodity shocks to consumer prices
impact.rangeExplainer: . Historically, realized inflation has been 55–75% of the ceiling due to government subsidies, price controls, currency hedging, and supply chain buffers. The typical range reflects this adjustment.

countrySimulator.title: Country Impact Simulator
countrySimulator.subtitle: Explore how conflict shocks affect a single country across all consumer categories. Toggle items, adjust assumptions, and see the full breakdown.

basket.title: Household Basics Basket
basket.subtitle: A weighted basket showing how conflict shocks combine across everyday goods. Toggle items on or off to see how the overall basket impact changes.
basket.formulaWeightedAvg: Weighted average = sum of (item weight / total active weight) x item impact across enabled items.
basket.formulaCpi: CPI contribution = sum of (item CPI weight / 100) x item impact. Measures percentage-point effect on headline CPI.
basket.disclaimerText: Basket weights are approximate CPI sub-index shares for {country}. Impact figures assume {pt}% pass-through under the {war} scenario with {lag} lag ({mult}x multiplier). Actual shelf prices will differ.

compare.title: Compare Scenarios
compare.subtitle: Select two war/shock scenarios with different assumptions and compare country-level impact side by side.
compare.noData: No data available for the selected scenarios.

saved.title: Saved Scenarios
saved.subtitle: Scenarios you have bookmarked for quick access. Data is stored locally in your browser.
saved.noSavedDesc: Use the simulator to explore war-impact scenarios and save the ones you want to revisit later.

badges.validatedDesc: Model ceiling has historically exceeded realized CPI changes in this market.
badges.indicativeDesc: Model may underestimate due to structural factors not fully captured.
badges.experimentalDesc: Limited validation data available. Treat estimates with caution.

shock.explainer: Factor shocks for the {war} scenario ({dates}) represent the peak-to-trough or baseline-to-peak price movement observed in major international benchmarks during the conflict window.
shock.caveat: These are point-in-time measurements, not forecasts. The model uses these as input shocks and applies country-specific exposure coefficients, FX adjustments, and lag profiles to estimate consumer price ceilings.

gate.invalidInput: Please enter a valid email or phone number.
gate.thankYouDesc: We will notify you of major model updates.

refinement.title: Refine Data
refinement.subtitle: You know your market better than our model does. Add what you know and we'll use it in the calculation — clearly labelled.
refinement.overridePrompt: Override the hardcoded price with what you know today.

soWhat.headline: How this conflict affects your daily costs
soWhat.noCountry: Select a country to see your personal impact
soWhat.topShocks: Key commodity shocks from this conflict
soWhat.basketImpact: Your household basics could cost {pct}% more under this scenario

presets.title: Quick Scenarios
presets.subtitle: See how different conflicts affect everyday costs

methodology.title: How the model works
methodology.subtitle: Full transparency on every assumption, coefficient, and data source that powers the impact estimates.

validation.title: Model validation
validation.subtitle: Comparing model ceiling estimates against realized CPI data to understand where the model performs well and where it falls short.
validation.fxStable: Model performs well where FX is stable
validation.validationContextDesc: Model ceilings shown above are computed at 100% pass-through with immediate lag (1.0x multiplier). In the simulator, selecting a different lag period (e.g., 12-month at 0.75x) will reduce the ceiling proportionally.

dataSources.title: Data sources
dataSources.subtitle: Every data point has a provenance. Here is where our numbers come from, how often they update, and what they cover.

about.title: About howwarimpactsyou.com
about.subtitle: A free, transparent tool that connects macro-level commodity and currency shocks to consumer-level price impacts.
about.missionTagline: Connecting macro shocks to consumer reality

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Questions, corrections, or ideas? We want to hear from you.

learn.title: Learning Hub
learn.subtitle: Understanding war's impact on prices
learn.deepDives: Deep dives into the economics behind conflict-driven price shocks.

data.title: Datasets & API
data.subtitle: All impact data is freely available for research, journalism, and public interest use.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: Every change to the model, data sources, and interface is documented here. Transparency is not just about the formula — it includes how the tool evolves over time.

press.title: Press Kit
press.subtitle: Resources for journalists, researchers, and media professionals.

flightAlerts.title: Flight Fuel Alert
flightAlerts.subtitle: Which countries face fuel shortages? How are flights affected? How many days of reserves remain?
flightAlerts.fuelCostShare: Fuel accounts for 20-30% of airline operating costs
flightAlerts.estimatedDepletion: Under full Hormuz disruption at current burn rate, reserves last approximately {days} days.
flightAlerts.notHormuzExposed: Minimal Hormuz dependency — not directly affected by strait disruption.
flightAlerts.flightRoutesSubtitle: Routes impacted by fuel shortages, rerouting, and surcharges under the {war} scenario.
flightAlerts.airlineImpactsSubtitle: How major airlines are responding to the fuel crisis.
flightAlerts.noDataForWar: No flight fuel data available for this scenario.
flightAlerts.dataAttribution: Oil data: EIA, IEA. Reserve data: JOGMEC, national reports. Flight data: IATA, airline press releases.
flightAlerts.digestUnavailable: Weekly digest unavailable. Check back later.
flightAlerts.newsAttribution: News: GDELT Project. Prices: U.S. EIA (public domain).
flightAlerts.heroSubtitleNew: Even preplanned trips are being hit. The Hormuz crisis is driving fuel shortages, flight cancellations, and surcharges across 26 countries. Find out if your route is next.
flightAlerts.routeCheckerSubtitle: Select your origin and destination to see fuel risk, estimated surcharges, and disruption status.
flightAlerts.verifiedExplainer: Based on actual airline and route data from verified sources.
flightAlerts.indicativeExplainer: Estimated from country-level fuel vulnerability profiles. Origin weighted 50-70%, layover 30% (if selected), destination 20-30%. Actual impact may vary.
flightAlerts.limitedExplainer: Insufficient data for one or both countries. Showing best available estimate.
flightAlerts.countriesSectionSubtitle: Fuel security scores for countries most exposed to the Hormuz disruption.
flightAlerts.timeFutureNote: Reserves deplete over time. Scores adjusted for {days} additional days of consumption at current disruption rates.
flightAlerts.ceasefireNote: Even with an immediate ceasefire, supply chains need weeks to normalize. Hormuz exposure reduced by {pct}%.
flightAlerts.returnTripNote: Assumes a 7-day stay. On the return leg, fuel is loaded at {country}. Reserves have depleted by 7 additional days.
flightAlerts.strandingHigh: High — {country} reserves may not support return fueling within 30 days
flightAlerts.strandingModerate: Moderate — {country} reserves are limited but currently adequate
flightAlerts.strandingLow: Low — {country} has adequate reserves for return fueling
flightAlerts.newsUnavailable: News articles are temporarily unavailable. Fuel price data shown below.
flightAlerts.leastImpactedSubtitle: Countries with low Hormuz exposure, strong reserves, or domestic production.
